what_it_does: Defines a React canvas component `Components` (window.Components, also exports window.COMPONENT_GROUPS) that is a gallery of UI patterns. It declares COMPONENT_GROUPS — built-in specs grouped Buttons/Inputs/Cards/Chips/Panels — and renders each pattern in a multi-state grid (default/hover/active/disabled and variant-specific states) via a hand-written `Renderer`. A generation panel calls window.CV_AI.complete with a prompt describing the brand CSS variables to produce one new component variant as inline-styled HTML (plus hover/disabled states), which the user can adopt; adopted/generated variants render their HTML via dangerouslySetInnerHTML.
observations: COMPONENT_GROUPS hardcodes the built-in catalog with id/name/desc per pattern (lines 9-38), each desc tying the pattern to real brand usage (e.g. MENU pill in the Virtual Hub viewer).
`generate()` prompt enumerates brand tokens (--accent-gold #E0C010, --accent-gold-soft, --accent-bronze #988058, radii, font) and asks for one variant with name/group/desc/html/states JSON.
Generated components render as HTML strings via dangerouslySetInnerHTML in ComponentStates/StateCell; built-ins render as real React in BuiltinStates/Renderer.
`BuiltinStates` maps each id to its own state list (e.g. check -> off/on, chip-status -> pending/success/error).
`Renderer` is a long if-chain that hand-renders each built-in id across states using inline styles with CSS vars.
Group filter pills include an extra 'Generated' bucket when session-generated variants exist.
Some demo values are inline hex literals (e.g. chip-task backgrounds #FBF1C0/#E6EFDB/#DCE7F3, badge red #F04325/#C24A3C, gradient #E8E2CC/#D0C098).
standouts: Two rendering paths in one gallery: real React for curated built-ins, raw injected HTML for AI-generated variants.
Component descriptions explicitly anchor each pattern to where it appears in the real product (Virtual Hub viewer, calendar, sidebar favourites).
AI generation returns inline-styled HTML keyed to the documented token vars so a new variant slots into the same state grid.
conventions: Follows a generate -> proposal -> adopt flow with discard.
Parses model JSON defensively (raw then {...} regex).
Uses CSS vars for built-in renderers; some demo-only color literals are inline.
Aliases React hooks with a `_cm` suffix for the shared bundle.
Built-in patterns are described as mirroring the patterns from the real UI kits (header comment).
concerns: Generated component HTML is rendered with dangerouslySetInnerHTML directly from model output, with no sanitization of the model-produced markup.
Renderer is a large hand-maintained if-chain that must be edited per new built-in id, separate from the COMPONENT_GROUPS data list.
Several status/demo colors are hardcoded hex rather than referencing the palette tokens.
notes: The component gallery: curated built-in patterns shown across states via hand-written React, plus a Vi panel that generates new inline-HTML variants in the brand token system.
questions: Are generated variants persisted beyond the session, and where does addGenerated store them?
Why are built-in patterns split between a data list (COMPONENT_GROUPS) and a separate Renderer if-chain rather than driven from one source?
Is the dangerouslySetInnerHTML on model output considered acceptable given the trusted-model assumption?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches. The header calls it a 'gallery of UI patterns with live state previews + Vi generation' and the component renders built-ins across states and generates new variants via CV_AI.
apparent_themes: Patterns anchored to real product usage
intention_signals: Trying to give a single browsable place to see every UI pattern in all its states and to extend the set by describing a new variant in natural language, returned as token-styled HTML that drops into the same state grid.
novelty: A gallery that mixes real-React built-in renderers with AI-generated inline-HTML variants in one state-grid presentation.
contribution: Contributes the Components canvas: a grouped catalog of built-in UI patterns with per-state previews and a Vi generator that emits adoptable token-styled HTML variants.
summary_for_embedding: A React canvas (window.Components) showing grouped built-in UI patterns across states via React renderers, plus a Vi generator that produces adoptable inline-styled HTML variants.
intention_for_embedding: To present every UI pattern in all its states and let users extend the component set by generating new brand-token-styled variants from a description.
